hero.newWay: A new way to lose weight
hero.title: Sustainable weight loss
hero.description: Our program combines approved medication with personal coaching from a dedicated care team to help you achieve and sustain a healthy weight.
hero.feature1: Effective weight loss up to 25-30 kg
hero.feature2: Reliable & approved medication and top ranked health coach assistance
hero.feature3: Seamless service – no lifelong subscriptions, no hidden costs
hero.feature4: Patients report top satisfaction and lasting change
hero.feature5: Easy to follow instructions & program to save your time & money

obesity.title: Understanding obesity:
obesity.titleHighlight: It's a complex medical condition, not a choice.
obesity.description: For too long, the story around weight has been about willpower. But modern medicine shows that obesity is a complex disease, deeply influenced by your genetics, hormones, and environment. We now understand the biology behind the struggle, and with that understanding comes new, effective medical treatments that can finally help.
obesity.bodyBiologyDescription: Your body's natural "set point" is controlled by hormones. When you diet, your body often fights back by increasing hunger and slowing your metabolism, making lasting results difficult on your own.
obesity.modernMedicineDescription: Clinically-proven medications like GLP-1s work with your body's biology. Under a doctor's guidance, they help adjust your set point and reduce hunger signals, making sustainable health possible.

pricing.subtitle: An Investment In Your Long-Term Health
pricing.title: Simple,
pricing.description: We believe in being completely open about the cost of our program. There are no hidden fees or long-term commitments. Your monthly membership gives you complete access to our medical team and the support you need to succeed on your journey.
pricing.doctorFeature: Receive ongoing care from a board-certified doctor who creates your plan, manages your prescriptions, and safely guides your progress.
pricing.medicationFeature: We manage the entire prescription process, with your approved medication prescribed to you and give you a personal health care coaching.
pricing.messagingFeature: Enjoy unlimited messaging with your personal care team for continuous support, coaching, and answers to all your questions.
pricing.noCommitmentFeature: There are no binding terms for your membership.
pricing.lifestyleDescription: Your all-in-one access to our medical treatment and personal lifestyle coaching team.
pricing.medicalDescription: Your access to medical treatment and support.
pricing.promoLabel: {{discount}} off for 6 months — use code at checkout
pricing.promoLabel3Months: {{discount}} off for 3 months — use code at checkout
pricing.maintenanceNote: Maintenance program pricing will be lower

features.unlimitedMessaging: Unlimited contact with your care team

faq.title: FAQ
faq.helpButton: Still need help? Get in touch
faq.introduction: Our program is straightforward, transparent, and supportive. You get medical expertise, personalized coaching, and ongoing follow-up — without unnecessary costs, complicated steps, or hidden conditions. We help you save time, build knowledge, and create long-term healthy lifestyle habits that truly last.

different.question: What makes your program different from other weight loss plans?
different.answer: Our program is built on transparency, medical expertise, and personal support. There are no hidden or unnecessary costs, and no obligations around prescriptions. Every plan is individualized — combining medical treatment when needed, regular lifestyle coaching (digital, personal, or in small groups), and ongoing follow-up. We focus on making the process simple and time-saving, so you don't have to chase appointments at your local healthcare center.

dietExercise.question: Do I still need to diet and exercise?
dietExercise.answer: Yes. Medication is not a replacement for healthy habits — it's a tool that makes it easier. We'll help you find realistic food and activity strategies that fit your life, without complicated steps or restrictive programs.

meetingFrequency.question: How often will I meet with the team?
meetingFrequency.answer: We offer regular check-ins, digitally. These sessions give you feedback, allow us to adjust your treatment, and provide lifestyle coaching so you feel supported at every step.

why.question: Why do you use medication for weight loss?
why.answer: Obesity is a medical condition that involves metabolism, hormones, and the brain's hunger signals. Medication helps by reducing cravings, balancing blood sugar, and supporting fullness. This makes lifestyle changes easier and more effective.

forever.question: Will I have to take the medication forever?
forever.answer: No. Some patients benefit from long-term treatment, while others use it for a shorter time to build new habits. The average treatment time is between 6 months and 2 years. Together, we'll review your progress and adjust the plan when the time is right.

whoCanTake.question: Who can take these medications?
whoCanTake.answer: Not everyone. You need to have BMI over 30, or BMI over 27 and one risk factor for developing cardiovascular disease (such as high blood pressure, high fasting blood sugar, high blood fats, apnea disease, etc). Safety is important, so we always start with a thorough medical review to make sure the treatment is right for you.

safe.question: Are these medications safe?
safe.answer: Yes — when used under professional medical supervision. We check your health carefully before starting and follow you closely throughout treatment.

alcohol.question: Can I drink alcohol while on treatment?
alcohol.answer: In most cases, low consumption of alcohol is safe. We'll give you tailored advice based on your health profile and treatment plan.

commonSideEffects.question: What are the most common side effects of medications for weight loss?
commonSideEffects.answer:
Many people experience digestive symptoms at the start, especially when doses are increased:
• Nausea or mild vomiting
• Constipation or diarrhea
• Feeling full more quickly than usual
• Bloating or mild stomach discomfort

These usually improve as your body adjusts to the medication. We'll guide you through how to manage these effects (for example, with dietary tips and gradual dose adjustments).

lesserCommonSideEffects.question: What are the less common but important side effects?
lesserCommonSideEffects.answer:
• Gallbladder issues: Rapid weight loss can sometimes increase the risk of gallstones.
• Pancreatitis (inflammation of the pancreas): Rare, but serious. Severe abdominal pain that doesn't go away needs urgent medical attention.
• Thyroid concerns: These medications aren't suitable for people with a personal or family history of certain rare thyroid cancers — we'll review your medical history to ensure safety.
• Muscle and bone loss: Quick weight loss can sometimes affect muscle and bone. That's why we'll support you with a nutrition and exercise plan to protect them.

expectations.question: What kind of results can I expect?
expectations.answer: Results vary from person to person. Many of our patients lose 10–20% of their body weight, sometimes as much as 25–30 kg, when combining medication with lifestyle changes and regular coaching.

howSoon.question: How soon will I notice results?
howSoon.answer: Most patients feel a reduced appetite within a few weeks. Weight loss usually starts within 1–2 months and continues gradually.

regain.question: Will I gain the weight back?
regain.answer: Weight regain can happen, but our long-term coaching and step-by-step medication tapering (usually 3–6 months) help reduce that risk. The goal is to build strong, sustainable habits that keep you at your new weight.

surgery.question: Do these medications replace surgery?
surgery.answer: For many patients, medication is enough. For others, it can be an alternative to, or part of, a surgical weight management journey.

noWeightLoss.question: What if I don't lose weight on the medication?
noWeightLoss.answer: We don't stop at one option. If results are limited, we'll adjust your dose, change your plan, or explore other treatment paths together.

bmi.title: Find out your BMI

tiers.notEligible: Our medical program requires a BMI of 27 or higher. Consider consulting your regular doctor for personalized advice.
tiers.conditional: You may qualify for treatment. A consultation is needed to assess your blood pressure and run blood tests to determine eligibility.
tiers.eligible: You are a candidate for treatment! Take the next step towards a healthier you.

bmi.disclaimer: *This is a starting point for understanding your health. If you have a BMI over 27, the next step is to speak with a medical professional.

step1.title: 1. Consultation
step1.description: Tell us about your health history in a short online form. You'll then have a consultation with a board-certified doctor to see if you're a candidate. To evaluate your health and ensure your treatment, you will need to provide blood samples and have your blood pressure measured at a laboratory. Once the test results are ready, your doctor will get back to you with prescriptions and a treatment plan.

step2.title: 2. Get Your Custom Plan
step2.description:
Your doctor reviews your health profile. You have two options to choose:

• **Medical treatment program**
A doctor evaluates your health and prescribes medication on a monthly basis. You receive continuous support and easy access to your care team through our chat function. We follow your progress regularly, monitor treatment results and side effects, and adjust the plan as needed until you reach your goal.

• **Lifestyle program**
Includes everything in the Medical Treatment Program, plus structured lifestyle support. You work with a health coach who guides you in nutrition, physical activity, and healthy routines. You participate in individual or group online sessions every other week for 6 months to help you build long‑lasting habits and achieve sustainable results.

step3.title: 3. Receive treatment and support all the way
step3.description:
Your dedicated care team provides ongoing support to guide you, adjust your plan, and celebrate your success.

We aim to treat you, give you the necessary support with lifestyle changes and educate you about overcoming obesity and having a healthy lifestyle. You receive your prescription online and can start your treatment. Enjoy your journey of losing weight and improving your health.

statistics.stat1: In Sweden, 1 in 2 adults is now classified as overweight, and 1 in 5 lives with obesity.
statistics.stat2: of people who lose weight through traditional dieting alone regain it within a few years.
statistics.stat3: Studies have shown that a 15% weight loss drastically improves heart health and reduces the risk of diabetes.

partner.title: About us!
partner.subtitle: Competence. Accessibility. Sustainable results
partner.intro: Losing weight isn't just about eating less or exercising more — it's about understanding your body and giving it the right support. At Vidacure, we help you take control of your health with a proven, medical approach to weight loss.

reasons.medical: Led by medical experts: Our team of doctors and health coaches work together to give you the best care possible.
reasons.science: Science you can trust: We use evidence-based treatments, including the latest weight-loss medications, combined with practical lifestyle strategies.
reasons.personalized: Personalized care: No two journeys are the same. We listen to you and design a plan that fits your life, your goals, and your needs.
reasons.longTerm: Long-term success: We focus not just on losing weight but on helping you keep it off — with ongoing support every step of the way.

partner.promiseText:
We know that living with obesity can be overwhelming. That's why we're here — to support you, guide you, and celebrate your progress. Whether through medication, coaching, or lifestyle changes, we'll help you achieve real, lasting results.

This isn't just another weight-loss program. It's your journey to better health, backed by a team who cares and the science that works.

Welcome to join our community!
Best Regards,

selma.name: Selma Mehr
selma.title: Specialist doctor in family medicine
selma.description: As a specialist in general medicine with eight years of experience, I have broad expertise in preventive healthcare and treatment of chronic diseases. My philosophy is to focus on a holistic approach to weight loss and health, as it's more than just diet and exercise. I work with patients to improve their metabolic control, thereby preventing diseases such as type 2 diabetes, high blood pressure, high blood lipids, and cardiovascular diseases.
selma.description2: My extensive experience also includes focus on stress management, sleep, and behavioral changes to achieve lasting results. I strive to make the entire process as personal and simple as possible, also offering health coaching as a vital part of the program.
selma.programDescription:
When you begin our medical treatment program, your first step is a digital consultation with a physician. During this meeting, we review your medical history, your individual circumstances, and your personal goals. If needed, you will be referred for blood tests to ensure that all treatment decisions are based on safe and accurate medical information.

Based on your results and needs, you receive a personalized treatment plan with clear instructions to support a safe and structured dose escalation. Throughout the entire process, we remain available for questions, guidance, and ongoing support.

Our goal is to guide you step by step until you reach a healthy and sustainable weight. Once your goal is achieved, we help you taper off the treatment gradually and safely.

Lifestyle changes are essential for long-term success. If you would like additional support, we recommend our lifestyle program, where you receive professional guidance from an experienced health coach.

marcus.name: Marcus Flink
marcus.title: Health Coach and Researcher
marcus.description: Marcus Flink is a licensed physiotherapist with a strong interest in longevity (living well, for a long time). As a physiotherapist, Marcus has worked with many different patient groups as well as with the Swedish national surf team. As a researcher, Marcus co-authored a nationwide study comparing digital group training with traditional group training for heart attack patients. The results show that digital groups are fully feasible, safe, and provide similar experiences and effects as traditional group training.
marcus.description2: The problem is that these digital group training sessions should have been available in heart patients' lives many years earlier — then perhaps the heart attack could have been avoided. Combined with knowledge of digital groups and Marcus's experience in health coaching, health-promoting interventions can now be implemented as soon as risk factors for future heart attacks are present. This means that people with high blood pressure, obesity, and physical inactivity should, and now can, receive the help that health coaching through digital groups offers.
marcus.coachingTitle: When you choose health coaching with Marcus
marcus.coachingDescription: When you choose to participate in health coaching with Marcus, you will be part of digital group sessions every two weeks for 6 months. Each session is structured to cover bio-, psycho-, and social factors that affect your weight and health. Individual goals will be designed and followed up on. To facilitate goal achievement, a community will be created regardless of where you are geographically. You will be given opportunities to share your journey and you will notice that you are not alone. Each group session ends with some form of movement and believe it or not, it will be fun! No requirement to be visible on camera, but it can help promote group dynamics.
marcus.bctDescription: Our treatment approach is based on Behaviour Change Techniques (BCT), a structured and research-based model for behaviour change. Through goal-focused work, continuous follow-up, and group support, we create the conditions for lasting lifestyle changes and sustainable weight loss.

giedre.name: Giedre Gaizauskaite-Poe
giedre.title: Rapid Transformational Therapy® Practitioner and Coach
giedre.description: My work focuses on serving people in finding the cause of their current limitations and together with my clients transforming them into a desired lifestyle and outcome. I have 14 years of experience working as a coach, I am trained in this incredible method called Rapid Transformational Therapy® and I am a hypnotherapist. I have selected this method to create change within people because it is quite simply the best method I have come across in all my practice.
giedre.description2: Lasting weight loss starts when we address the root causes, not just the symptoms. If you're here, it's because you're ready for real change. Not a quick fix, not another short-term plan, but a lifestyle you can truly sustain — one that supports your health, your energy, and your future.
giedre.description3: Perhaps you've even struggled with your weight for years, doing "everything right" yet still feeling stuck. And deep down, you may sense that it's not only about food or exercise. Our habits are often shaped by emotions, experiences, and patterns we don't even realize we're carrying. Does that resonate with you?
giedre.description4: This is why I invite you to look beyond the surface and explore the root causes behind your current behaviors — so you can gently release what no longer serves you and create new habits that last. With the right support, you can build the conditions for lasting change and enjoy better health, confidence, and well-being.
giedre.programSubtitle: Rewire your mind for sustainable weight loss
giedre.programDescription: I have designed a Mind-Body Recode Program to help you create permanent changes so that you can get the best results on your weight loss journey. It consists of a few elements and depending on how we choose to use the time it could extend to 6–8 weeks approximately.
programIncludes: 1 tailor-made 100% individualized audio recording to reflect our session, a recording you will listen to daily
2 × 1-hour coaching sessions towards your bright future
1-hour coaching and 30-min call at the end of the cycle

partner.ctaText: Ready to start your health journey?

testimonials.testimonial1: If there is anywhere you should turn for help, it is here!! I have now lost 28 kilos and feel fitter, stronger and the self-confidence that was at rock bottom has completely turned around. Doctor Selma is knowledgeable, empathetic and a person who cares incredibly much during your health journey. You will get an incredible amount of tips, advice and a doctor who cheers you on throughout your journey.
testimonials.testimonial2: I really appreciate the clear and easy-to-understand instructions regarding the medication, which have made it simple to follow the treatment. In addition, the valuable advice on how to change my habits around diet and exercise has given me motivation and tools to improve my health in the long term. I can highly recommend Vidacure for their great commitment and support.
testimonials.testimonial3: I truly want to recommend Vidacure and my doctor, Selma. I thought surgery was my last resort for weight loss — until she encouraged me to try the medication. I needed to lose 30 kg, and within 10 months I reached my goal. I could never have done it without this support. I always get quick responses to my questions and concerns. Eternally grateful that I found my way here!
testimonials.testimonial4: I want to express my deepest gratitude for the support I have received from Dr. Selma — an exceptional and deeply insightful physician who has truly changed my life. When I first began my medical treatment, I was uncertain and had many questions, but from the very beginning, Dr. Selma was clear about what I could expect. She carefully explained that the medication is not a final solution, but rather a tool — a starting point for making sustainable changes in my lifestyle. Throughout the entire process, she has been a constant source of support and inspiration, and with her guidance, I have managed to lose 20 kg. But it's not just the weight loss that has made a difference. Dr. Selma has given me a true understanding of what it takes to make lasting changes that will carry me forward. Now, 20 kg lighter and with a completely new outlook on my health, I no longer see the treatment as the end of my journey. Thank you for everything, Dr. Selma. Thank you for believing in me — and for helping me believe in myself.

article1.title: What is Obesity?
article1.content:
Obesity is a chronic and complex disease that affects the body’s ability to regulate weight. It is not only about food and physical activity, but a combination of biological, genetic, hormonal and environmental factors.
BMI is often used to describe obesity, but it is only one of several tools. Waist circumference, body composition and individual risk factors provide a more complete picture.
Obesity increases the risk of several related conditions such as type 2 diabetes, high blood pressure, sleep apnea and cardiovascular disease. At the same time, there are many effective ways to treat and manage the condition, and the right support can make a significant difference over time.
## Why does obesity occur?
Obesity develops when the body’s systems for hunger, satiety and energy balance become dysregulated. Many people believe that weight gain is about lack of discipline, but research shows that biological factors play a major role.
Hormones such as leptin and ghrelin influence how hungry we feel and how the body defends a certain weight. Genetic factors can make some individuals more prone to gaining weight than others.
The environment also plays a role. Stress, lack of sleep, sedentary work and easily accessible energy-dense foods make it harder to maintain a stable weight. Psychological factors such as emotional eating and habits also contribute.
Obesity is therefore the result of many interacting mechanisms, not a personal failure.
## How is obesity treated?
Treatment of obesity is based on a combination of lifestyle changes, behavioral support and medical assessment. The goal is to create sustainable habits and improve long-term health.
Lifestyle treatment includes dietary patterns, physical activity, sleep and stress management. Psychological methods in the form of supportive conversations can help change behaviors and create structure.
There are also medical and surgical treatment options that may be appropriate for some individuals.
Digital tools can provide support through education, self-monitoring and continuous coaching, making it easier to follow one’s plan in everyday life.
## How is the right treatment chosen?
The right treatment for obesity depends on a comprehensive picture of the person’s health, life situation and goals. A medical assessment may include blood tests, risk profiling, comorbidities and previous experiences with weight change.
The treatment is then tailored to the individual’s needs. Some primarily need support with behavioral change, others need help with structure and motivation, and some may require medical treatment as part of the overall plan.
The most important factor is that the treatment is individualized and followed up regularly.
## Lifelong weight stability
Losing weight is one thing, but maintaining weight loss is often the biggest challenge. The body tends to return to its previous weight, which makes long-term support especially important.
Helpful strategies include regular routines, self-monitoring, planning, stress management and having a clear structure in daily life. Relapses are common and a natural part of the process. What matters is returning to healthy habits as soon as possible.
With the right support, weight stability can become both achievable and sustainable.
## Digital support in obesity treatment
Digital tools have become an important part of modern obesity treatment. They make it easier to follow one’s plan, track habits and get help when needed.
A digital service can offer education, coaching, behavioral support, self-monitoring and reminders that help create structure. For many people, it is a flexible and accessible way to receive continuous support in everyday life.
Digital treatment does not replace medical care, but it can be a valuable complement for achieving long-term change.
## Our method and philosophy
Our method is based on evidence-based care and a holistic understanding of obesity as a chronic disease. As licensed healthcare professionals, we provide medical assessment, treatment and follow-up in accordance with current clinical guidelines.
We begin with a medical evaluation where we review health status, risk factors, blood tests and individual goals. Based on this, we create a treatment plan that may include lifestyle support, behavior change, digital coaching and, when medically appropriate, pharmacological treatment.
Medication is used only when clinically indicated and after an individual assessment. We always provide information in a neutral and factual manner and follow all regulations regarding medical and pharmaceutical communication.
Our focus is long-term health. We work with continuous follow-up, education and support to help you build sustainable habits and understand how your body functions. The goal is for you to feel safe, involved and supported throughout the entire process.

article2.title: Treating Obesity: What You Need to Know
article2.content:
Obesity is a chronic and complex disease that affects hormonal balance, metabolism, fertility and long-term health. Research shows that obesity is not about a lack of discipline, but about biological mechanisms that affect hunger, satiety, energy expenditure and hormonal regulation. For this reason, medical assessment and structured treatment are recommended in the same way as for other chronic diseases.
Below is a summary of treatment principles based on international guidelines and research:
## Lifestyle treatment – the foundation of all obesity treatment
Lifestyle changes are a central part of treatment and form the basis of every guideline.
### Diet
Research shows that several dietary patterns can improve weight and metabolic factors. Common denominators are:
• higher intake of protein and fibre
• slow-release carbohydrates
• reduced intake of ultra-processed food
• regular meals
Structured dietary programmes can improve blood sugar regulation and insulin resistance.
### Physical activity
Physical activity is important for health and contributes to:
• improved insulin sensitivity
• better blood pressure
• improved sleep
• reduced stress
• greater weight stability after weight loss
### Behavioural support
Support from healthcare professionals or digital programmes can:
• strengthen motivation
• create sustainable routines
• reduce the risk of relapse
## Medical Treatment: When Lifestyle Changes Are Not Enough
International guidelines state that medical treatment may be considered for individuals with:
• BMI ≥ 30
• BMI ≥ 27 with comorbidities (e.g., insulin resistance, PCOS, type 2 diabetes, sleep apnoea or high blood pressure)
In Sweden, all medications used for obesity treatment are prescription-only and require individual assessment by licensed healthcare professionals.
### Medication Groups
There are currently several types of medications used in the treatment of overweight and obesity, including:
• medications that influence appetite regulation
• medications that improve insulin sensitivity
• medications that reduce energy absorption in the gut
Effect and suitability vary between individuals, and treatment must always be prescribed and monitored by healthcare professionals.
## Surgical Treatment – for Severe Obesity or When Other Methods Are Insufficient
Bariatric surgery, such as gastric bypass or gastric sleeve, may be considered for individuals with very high BMI (≥ 40) or BMI ≥ 35 with comorbidities, and can be a possible option for certain individuals.
## Obesity, Fertility and Pregnancy – Why Treatment Matters
Treatment of obesity may contribute to:
• improved ovulation
• increased chance of pregnancy
• reduced risk of gestational diabetes
• reduced risk of high blood pressure and preeclampsia
• improved outcomes in assisted reproduction
• reduced risk of complications during childbirth
Studies show that even moderate weight loss (5–10%) can improve hormonal balance and fertility.
## Summary
Obesity is a chronic disease that requires long-term and individualised treatment. The most effective approach often consists of a combination of:
• lifestyle support
• medical assessment and, when appropriate, medication
• in some cases, surgery
Treatment can improve weight, hormonal balance, fertility, pregnancy outcomes and long-term health.
## Sources
• Mechanisms of obesity as a chronic disease. Nature Reviews Endocrinology. PubMed
• Pharmacologic treatment of obesity: an Endocrine Society guideline. Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism. PubMed
• GLP-1 receptor agonists for weight management: clinical evidence. Lancet Diabetes & Endocrinology. PubMed
• Bariatric surgery and long-term outcomes. JAMA.
• Obesity and fertility: impact on ovulation and pregnancy outcomes. Human Reproduction Update. PubMed
• PCOS, insulin resistance and metabolic dysfunction. Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism. PubMed
• Weight loss and fertility improvement in women with PCOS. Human Reproduction. PubMed

article3.title: How Overweight and Obesity Affect Women's Health
article3.content:
## Pregnancy and fertility in the context of obesity
Obesity can affect both fertility and pregnancy in several ways. Hormonal changes, insulin resistance and irregular ovulation can make it take longer to conceive. Many people find that their menstrual cycle becomes more regular when insulin resistance improves, which in turn can increase the chance of ovulation.
During pregnancy, the risk of certain complications increases if a person has obesity. These may include gestational diabetes, high blood pressure, preeclampsia and greater physical strain on the body during pregnancy. Labour can also be affected, for example through an increased likelihood of caesarean section or a longer labour process.
It is important to remember that many people with obesity do become pregnant and have healthy babies. With medical assessment, support for lifestyle changes and regular follow-up, risks can be reduced and the conditions for a healthy pregnancy can be improved. For those planning a pregnancy, even small improvements in health and weight can have a positive impact on fertility and pregnancy outcomes.
## PCOS and insulin resistance
Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is one of the most common hormonal conditions among women of reproductive age. Many people are familiar with symptoms such as irregular periods, increased hair growth or difficulty becoming pregnant, but fewer know how strongly PCOS is linked to the body’s ability to handle insulin. Here we will go through what insulin resistance is, why it is common in PCOS and what you can do to support your health.
### What is insulin resistance
Insulin is a hormone that helps the body absorb sugar from the bloodstream and use it as energy. When the cells do not respond properly to insulin, the condition is called insulin resistance. The body then tries to compensate by producing more insulin, which leads to higher insulin levels in the blood.
Elevated insulin levels can affect several functions in the body, including hormone balance. In people with PCOS, this can contribute to increased levels of androgens (male-type hormones such as testosterone), which in turn may cause symptoms such as acne, increased hair growth and irregular ovulation.
### Why insulin resistance is common in PCOS
Research shows that a large proportion of people with PCOS have insulin resistance, even those who are normal weight. This means that insulin resistance is not only linked to excess weight, but to the underlying disease mechanism itself.
There are several explanations for this connection:
• genetic factors can influence how the body responds to insulin
• hormonal imbalances in PCOS can make cells less sensitive to insulin
• high insulin levels can increase androgen production in the ovaries
• androgens can in turn worsen insulin resistance
This creates a vicious cycle where insulin resistance and hormonal imbalance reinforce each other. This is why many people with PCOS experience symptoms such as fatigue after meals, sugar cravings, weight gain around the abdomen or difficulty losing weight.
### How to recognise insulin resistance
Insulin resistance often causes vague symptoms that can be easy to miss. Common signs include:
• energy dips after meals
• strong sugar cravings
• weight gain despite unchanged habits
• difficulty losing weight
• increased hunger
• darker, velvety skin patches in body folds, known as acanthosis nigricans
Symptoms vary, and many people notice nothing at all. This is why medical assessment and blood tests are often necessary to get a clear picture.
### How insulin resistance is assessed in PCOS
An evaluation may include fasting glucose, HbA1c, lipid profile or, in some cases, an oral glucose tolerance test. It is rarely a single test that provides the answer — instead, the overall picture is assessed.
### How to improve insulin resistance
There are several ways to positively influence insulin resistance, and small changes can make a big difference over time. Eating at regular times helps keep blood sugar stable. A combination of protein, fibre and slow-release carbohydrates helps prevent rapid blood sugar spikes. Physical activity increases the cells’ sensitivity to insulin, and even short walks after meals can be helpful. High stress levels can affect both blood sugar and hormones. Good sleep is important for hormone balance and the body’s recovery.
### Medical treatment
In some cases, medication that improves insulin sensitivity may be appropriate. This is always assessed individually by licensed healthcare professionals.
### Why it is important to treat insulin resistance in PCOS
Improving insulin resistance can reduce androgen levels, improve menstrual regularity, increase the chance of ovulation, lower the risk of type 2 diabetes and improve energy levels and overall wellbeing.
Many people find that their symptoms become easier to manage when insulin resistance improves, even though PCOS itself is a chronic condition.
## Summary
PCOS and insulin resistance are closely linked. Insulin resistance is very common in PCOS and can affect both hormone balance and symptoms. It is not a sign of poor discipline or wrong choices, but a biological mechanism that can be influenced with the right support. Through lifestyle changes, and in some cases medical treatment and continuous follow-up, it is possible to improve insulin sensitivity and feel better in the long term.
## Sources
• International Evidence Based Guideline for the Assessment and Management of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome, Monash University, 2023
• American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists, Practice Bulletin on PCOS
• Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline on PCOS
• Johns Hopkins Medicine, Overview of Insulin Resistance
• Polycystic ovary syndrome and excessive body weight impact independently and synergically on fertility treatment outcomes. Reproductive Biology and Endocrinology. 2025.
• Correlation analysis of BMI with ovulation effect and clinical pregnancy rate in patients with PCOS. American Journal of Translational Research. 2024.
• Obesity as a Part of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) — A Review of Pathophysiology and Comprehensive Therapeutic Strategies. Journal of Clinical Medicine. 2025.
• Association between BMI, weight loss and the chance of pregnancy in women with PCOS. Human Reproduction. 2023.

article4.title: Nutrition and Obesity: What the Research Says
article4.content:
Research shows that the composition of the diet affects blood sugar, insulin resistance, hunger, satiety and weight regulation. It is not about strict diets or restrictions, but about creating balance, regularity and nutrient-dense choices that work in everyday life.
## 1. Regular meals create stability for blood sugar and hunger
Irregular eating patterns can lead to:
• strong hunger signals
• fluctuating blood sugar
• increased risk of overeating
• fatigue and sugar cravings
Regularity helps the body recognize hunger and satiety, which is especially important in obesity where these signals are often affected.
## 2. Protein and fiber are key to satiety and stable energy
Protein and fiber are central components of nutritional therapy in obesity. They contribute to:
• increased satiety
• slower blood sugar rise
• reduced cravings
• preserved muscle mass during weight loss
Examples of good sources:
• fish, eggs, chicken, tofu, beans, vegetables, legumes, whole grains, fruit
## 3. Slow-release carbohydrates provide steadier energy and fewer cravings
Slow carbohydrates help to:
• stabilize blood sugar
• reduce energy dips
• improve concentration and stamina
Good examples include oats, whole grains, legumes and root vegetables.
## 4. Ultra-processed foods affect the body more than you think
Ultra-processed foods are often:
• energy-dense
• low in fiber
• quick to eat
• engineered to trigger the reward system
This makes it harder for the body to regulate hunger and satiety.
## 5. Eating behaviors are an important part of treatment
In obesity, hunger and satiety signals often function differently. Therefore, behavioral support is an important part of nutritional treatment.
Effective strategies may include:
• eating more slowly
• planning meals
• reducing distractions during meals
• identifying stress- or emotion-driven eating
## 6. Biological factors matter — which is why nutrition is not the whole solution
In obesity, the following are affected:
• hunger hormones
• satiety hormones
• insulin sensitivity
• the brain’s reward system
This means the body often resists weight loss, even with good dietary habits. Medical assessment and complementary treatment may therefore be appropriate, always based on an individual evaluation by licensed healthcare professionals.
## 7. The role of nutrition in fertility and pregnancy
For individuals with obesity, improved dietary habits can contribute to:
• more regular ovulation
• reduced insulin resistance
• lower risk of gestational diabetes
• improved IVF outcomes
## Summary: Nutrition in obesity is about balance, not perfection
Nutrition in obesity focuses on:
• establishing regular meals
• prioritizing protein and fiber
• choosing slow-release carbohydrates
• reducing ultra-processed foods
• understanding eating behaviors
• respecting the body’s biology

article5.title: Exercise for Overweight and Obesity
article5.content:
Exercise is a key component in the treatment of overweight and obesity and plays a major role in weight management, metabolic health and long-term wellbeing. Physical activity does far more than increase calorie expenditure. It improves hormonal balance, builds muscle mass, enhances insulin sensitivity and supports a stable metabolism. For individuals living with overweight, exercise becomes an essential tool for weight loss, weight maintenance and improved metabolic function.
## How the body responds to physical activity in overweight
Carrying excess weight can make exercise feel challenging, but the physiological benefits are substantial. Regular physical activity improves blood pressure, blood glucose, sleep quality and mental wellbeing. Exercise also reduces low-grade inflammation, a key mechanism in obesity. For many people, movement becomes a way to regain control of their health and build a more active lifestyle.
## Choosing the right type of exercise for weight management
The most effective exercise is the one that can be maintained consistently. Walking, cycling and swimming are accessible forms of moderate-intensity activity that provide significant health benefits. Strength training is particularly valuable because increased muscle mass raises basal energy expenditure and supports long-term weight maintenance. Adapting exercise to daily energy levels, joint health and medical needs is essential for sustainability.
## Exercise as part of evidence-based obesity treatment
Exercise is most effective when combined with nutritional strategies, behavioral support and medical assessment. The goal is consistency rather than perfection. Even small amounts of regular movement improve health outcomes and increase the likelihood of long-term weight stability. When exercise becomes a natural part of daily life, it transforms into an investment in quality of life, vitality and future wellbeing.
## Sources
• Battista F, Ermolao A, van Baak M, et al. Effect of exercise on cardiometabolic health of adults with overweight or obesity: Focus on blood pressure, insulin resistance, and intrahepatic fat — A systematic review and meta-analysis. Obesity Reviews. 2021. [Full text (PMC)](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8365642/)
• Liu Y, Wang X, Fang Z. Evaluating the impact of exercise on intermediate disease markers in overweight and obese individuals through a network meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials. Scientific Reports. 2024. [Full text (PMC)](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC11130208/)
• Smart NA, King N, McFarlane JR, Graham PL, Dieberg G. Effect of exercise training on liver function in adults who are overweight or exhibit fatty liver disease: A systematic review and meta-analysis. British Journal of Sports Medicine. [Article](https://bjsm.bmj.com/content/52/13/834)

article6.title: GLP-1 Treatment for Overweight: The Difference Between Semaglutide and Tirzepatide (Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro)
article6.content:
An evidence-based guide from Vidacure
GLP-1–based medications have become some of the most studied and effective tools in the treatment of overweight and obesity. By influencing the body’s hunger and satiety systems, these medications can help patients achieve significant weight loss as part of a comprehensive medical treatment plan. In this article, we describe how semaglutide, found in Ozempic and Wegovy, and tirzepatide, found in Mounjaro, work in the body and what differentiates them.
## How Do GLP-1 Medications Work in the Body?
GLP-1 is a natural hormone released in the intestine after a meal. It affects several key functions in the body. It increases the feeling of fullness and reduces hunger and cravings, while also slowing the rate at which the stomach empties. GLP-1 also stabilizes blood sugar levels by increasing insulin secretion and lowering levels of glucagon, a hormone that otherwise raises blood sugar. When these effects work together, they often lead to reduced energy intake, which can contribute to weight loss.
## Semaglutide: Ozempic and Wegovy
Semaglutide is a GLP-1 agonist available in two different medications. Ozempic is approved for the treatment of type 2 diabetes but is sometimes used off-label for overweight. Clinical studies have shown that Ozempic can lead to an average weight loss of around 10–15 percent of body weight. Wegovy contains the same active substance but in higher doses and is specifically approved for the treatment of obesity and overweight. Studies show that Wegovy can result in approximately 15 percent weight loss. Semaglutide primarily affects appetite regulation and satiety, making it easier for many patients to reduce their calorie intake.
## Tirzepatide: Mounjaro
Tirzepatide is a newer medication that differs from semaglutide by acting on two hormonal systems simultaneously. In addition to activating GLP-1, it also activates GIP, a hormone that enhances insulin secretion and further influences appetite regulation. This dual mechanism often results in greater weight loss than semaglutide. Mounjaro is approved for the treatment of obesity in Sweden. In clinical studies, tirzepatide has led to weight loss of around 20–22 percent of body weight, which is higher than what has been observed with semaglutide.
## Comparison Between Semaglutide and Tirzepatide
Semaglutide, found in Ozempic and Wegovy, acts solely through GLP-1, while tirzepatide in Mounjaro affects both GLP-1 and GIP. Wegovy and Mounjaro are approved for weight management, while Ozempic is approved for diabetes treatment. The appetite-reducing effect is strong with semaglutide but even more pronounced with tirzepatide, which is reflected in clinical results showing approximately 10–15 percent weight loss with semaglutide and around 20–22 percent with tirzepatide. Both medications are administered as weekly injections.
## Why Does Tirzepatide Often Work Better?
The dual effect on both GLP-1 and GIP provides the body with a stronger satiety signal and more efficient blood sugar regulation. This can lead to a more noticeable reduction in hunger, more stable energy levels, and a more consistent calorie deficit. Together, these mechanisms contribute to tirzepatide often producing greater overall weight loss than semaglutide in clinical studies.
## Are GLP-1 Medications Right for Everyone?
GLP-1 treatment is not suitable for everyone. Treatment with semaglutide or tirzepatide always requires a medical assessment, regular monitoring of effects and side effects, and should be combined with lifestyle support. Vidacure follows evidence-based guidelines and adheres to regulatory standards to ensure that all information is neutral, accurate, and safe for patients.
## Side effects and price differences
Both medications work on incretin hormones and therefore share many gastrointestinal side effects. However, the frequency and intensity differ slightly.
### Semaglutide (Wegovy)
Semaglutide’s side effects are well-documented and mostly gastrointestinal. The most common include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, abdominal discomfort, and reduced appetite. These effects are usually strongest during dose escalation and tend to decrease over time. Some patients experience acid reflux or bloating. Rare but serious risks include gallbladder issues and pancreatitis.
### Tirzepatide (Mounjaro)
Tirzepatide has a similar side-effect profile but can cause slightly more gastrointestinal symptoms at higher doses, likely due to its dual activation of both GLP-1 and GIP receptors. Nausea, diarrhea, and decreased appetite are common. Some patients report fatigue or mild injection-site reactions. As with semaglutide, gallbladder issues and pancreatitis are rare but possible.
### Price comparison
In many markets, Wegovy costs slightly less than Mounjaro. Mounjaro is often more expensive than Wegovy. In Sweden, Mounjaro is approved for obesity, but pricing is still high and it is not reimbursed.
## Summary
Semaglutide, found in Ozempic and Wegovy, and tirzepatide, found in Mounjaro, influence the body’s hunger and satiety systems in different ways. Wegovy and Mounjaro are approved for weight management, while Ozempic is approved for diabetes. Tirzepatide has shown greater weight-loss effects in studies due to its dual mechanism of action. Regardless of medication, treatment requires medical follow-up and should always be combined with lifestyle support for the best results.
| Category | Semaglutide (Wegovy) | Tirzepatide (Mounjaro) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Mechanism | GLP-1 agonist | Dual GIP + GLP-1 agonist |
| GI side effects | Common | Slightly more common |
| Other side effects | Similar profile | Similar profile |
| Discontinuation rates | Similar | Similar |
| Weight loss | ~15% | ~20–22% |
| Price | High | Slightly higher |
| Cost-effectiveness | Good | Often better due to greater weight loss |
## Sources
• SURMOUNT-5 (Head-to-head RCT: tirzepatide vs semaglutide in obesity). This is the most important comparison study. [PMID: 39651455](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39651455/)
• Real-world comparative effectiveness (JAMA Internal Medicine). Large cohort study (18,386 matched patients). [PMID: 39504067](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39504067/)
• Mechanistic review: GLP-1 vs GIP/GLP-1 dual agonists. Explains why tirzepatide works better (dual incretin activation). [PMID: 39871635](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39871635/)
• Additional mechanistic and clinical comparison review. A narrative review summarizing SURPASS, SURMOUNT, and semaglutide trials. [PMID: 39631030](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39631030/)

privacyPolicy.seoDescription: Read about how Albafides Care AB (Vidacure) processes your personal data in accordance with GDPR and the Swedish Patient Data Act.
privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.intro: Albafides Care AB, reg. no. 551929-4888, with address Stora Varvsgatan 15E, 211 77 Malmö, is responsible for the processing of personal data within the scope of our operations and digital services under the brand Vidacure. We comply with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the Swedish Patient Data Act, and other relevant legislation.
privacyPolicy.dpoText: Our Data Protection Officer helps ensure that we handle personal data correctly and securely. You can contact the DPO via email: dpo@vidacure.se or by mail to Albafides Care AB, attn: Data Protection Officer, Stora Varvsgatan 15E, 211 77 Malmö.

section1.title: 1. What personal data we process
section1.intro: Personal data is information that can be directly or indirectly linked to you as an individual. This may include:
examples: Name, personal identity number, and contact details
Information about health, lifestyle, and medical history
Technical data from our digital services
section1.processingDef: Processing of personal data includes all actions relating to the data, such as collection, storage, structuring, analysis, transfer, and deletion.
section1.legalBasisIntro: To process personal data, we must have a legal basis under GDPR. For our operations, the following are primarily used:
legalBases: Contract – when you use our services
Legal obligation – e.g., record-keeping under the Patient Data Act
Legitimate interest – e.g., to improve our services
Consent – when required for specific purposes
section1.sensitiveNote: When processing sensitive personal data, such as health data, we rely on GDPR Article 9, specifically 9.2(h) (healthcare) and 9.2(a) (explicit consent).

section2.title: 2. How we process personal data in different situations

subsections.title: 2.1 When you create an account and we assess if the service is suitable for you
subsections.examples: Name, personal identity number, age, contact details, health information, lifestyle data, photos, technical data, user data.
processing: Collection of data via forms, calls, and digital services
Assessment of whether Vidacure is suitable for you
subsections.purpose: To offer an account, carry out medical assessments, and provide our digital services.

subsections.title: 2.2 When you subscribe and pay for a membership
subsections.examples: Personal identity number, name, contact details, payment information, credit assessment.
subsections.recipients: Payment providers, IT providers, BankID, credit reference agencies.

subsections.title: 2.3 When we provide care and treatment
subsections.examples: Health data, test results, medical records, lifestyle data, communication with healthcare staff, photos, user data.
processing: Communication between you, healthcare staff, and other patients (e.g., community features)
subsections.legalBasis: Contract and legal obligation under the Patient Data Act.
subsections.recipients: Laboratories, pharmacies, other healthcare providers, IT providers, prescription services, authorities when required.

subsections.title: 2.4 When we work on patient safety and quality improvement
subsections.examples: Health data, user data, incident management, injury investigations.
processing: Handling of IVO (Health and Social Care Inspectorate) cases
subsections.legalBasis: Legitimate interest and legal obligations in healthcare.

subsections.title: 2.5 When you contact customer service
subsections.examples: Contact details, personal identity number, technical data, information you provide in the matter.

subsections.title: 2.6 When we market our services
subsections.examples: Contact details, user behavior, reviews, information you provide in recommendations or "refer a friend".
subsections.legalBasis: Legitimate interest and consent where required.

subsections.title: 2.7 When we develop and improve our services
subsections.examples: Technical data, user behavior, comments, health data (which is anonymized).

items.title: 3.1 Employees and consultants
items.text: Only individuals who need the data for their work are given access.

items.title: 3.2 Data processors
items.text: We engage providers for IT operations, customer service, marketing, laboratory services, etc. With all of these, we have agreements on data protection, confidentiality, and security, ensuring they only process data according to our instructions and with a high level of protection.

items.title: 3.3 Independent data controllers
items.text: In some cases, data is shared with entities that are independently responsible for their own processing, such as authorities (Swedish Tax Agency, IVO, Swedish Social Insurance Agency, Police), pharmacies, laboratories, payment providers, and other healthcare providers. When data is shared with these parties, their own privacy policies apply.

section4.title: 4. How long we retain data
items: Medical records: 10 years under the Patient Data Act.
Contract and subscription data: during the contract period + 6 months.
Marketing data: up to 6 months after the activity ends.
Data based on consent: deleted upon withdrawal or after 2 years at the latest.
Data for development and research: anonymized.

section5.title: 5. Where data is processed
section5.text: We strive to process all personal data within the EU/EEA. If data is transferred to countries outside the EU/EEA, we ensure the level of protection through EU Commission adequacy decisions, or standard contractual clauses and other appropriate safeguards.

section6.title: 6. Your rights
rights: Receive information about how we process your data
Request access to your data (subject access request)
Object to processing based on legitimate interest
Receive your data for data portability
section6.contact: You can contact us or our Data Protection Officer to exercise your rights.
section6.complaint: You may also file a complaint with the Swedish Authority for Privacy Protection (IMY).
